pattern: Assigning digits column by column from the units place with a carry lets the addition constraints prune the search early, so backtracking over at most ten letters stays tractable.
hints: There are at most ten distinct letters, so a digit assignment is a partial permutation of 0 through 9 - small enough to search with backtracking.
Work the columns from least significant to most significant, carrying like manual addition, so each column gives an equation that constrains the letters in it before you go deeper.

Forbid a zero for any leading letter, ensure no two letters share a digit, and at the final column require the running carry to vanish; if any complete assignment satisfies every column, the puzzle is solvable.

edge_inputs.description: classic two-word puzzle that is solvable
result too short to ever hold the sum
leading-zero restriction blocks the only sum

stress.description: small random puzzles within a tight letter budget so the search stays fast
stress.note: tight caps keep the column backtracking fast on every generated case
